user_prompt: the exact same thing as the previous instruction but using the motor control pwm
agent_reply: It uses ESP-IDF MCPWM on GPIO14, requesting `8192000 Hz` with a two-tick period and compare at one tick, so 50% duty in the lowest useful PWM resolution shape. It prints each setup step, starts the timer, then idles forever like the other hardware probes.
Validation: `pio run -e thefly_sec0` passes, and I cleaned up the generated `src/version.c` churn afterward.
Small hardware note: ESP32 MCPWM is fed from `PLL160M` through integer prescalers, so watch serial for an MCPWM “adjust timer resolution” warning and trust the scope for the actual output frequency.
